Summary: [BE] requireRider skips is_approved on 3 of 6 endpoints
Description: deliveryPartner.controller.ts:28-58 — requireRider only validates the session token exists/isn't expired. acceptOrder/acceptOffer individually check is_approved, but rejectOrder/markPickedUp/markDelivered don't — so a pending_verification rider signed up seconds ago can still call those three.

Summary: [BE] supabaseAdmin silently falls back to anon client
Description: config/database.ts:61-62 — export const supabaseAdmin = supabaseServiceKey ? createClient(...) : supabase; JWT-role validation logs loud errors on a bad key, but the fallback itself is unchanged: any DB operation meant to bypass RLS instead silently runs as anon and gets RLS-filtered, rather than failing closed. Given how many critical paths depend on supabaseAdmin (checkout, payments, admin), a misconfigured key degrades to confusing empty-result bugs instead of a loud startup failure.

Summary: [BE] storeOwner.routes.ts has no router-level auth
Description: Unlike every other role's router. deleteStoreProduct (103-151) looks up app_users by session token without filtering role='shopkeeper', unlike other handlers in the same file — inconsistent auth pattern, latent IDOR if stores.owner_id is ever set programmatically for a non-shopkeeper.

Summary: [BE] cancelOrder has no order-status guard
Description: database.service.ts:419-506 only blocks cancellation if a delivery partner is assigned — never checks if the order is already order_delivered. A delivered order can be cancelled and auto-refunded (payment_status === 'paid' triggers processRefund).

Summary: [BE] Admin updateOrderStatus has no state-machine check
Description: orders.controller.ts:205-265 — any admin session can move order_delivered back to pending_at_store, re-triggering customer notifications inappropriately.

Summary: [BE] Tracking endpoints leak PII cross-customer
Description: tracking.controller.ts — getOrderTracking, getOrderTrackingFull, getDriverLocationsForOrder, getTrackingHistory never check the requested orderId belongs to req.customerId, despite being requireCustomer-gated. Any logged-in customer can view any other customer's delivery address + rider phone/location by guessing/observing an order UUID.

Summary: [BE] Riders can spoof GPS / write arbitrary order status
Description: tracking.controller.ts:107-122 (updateAgentLocation) never checks agentId === req.riderId. addTrackingUpdate (64-87) accepts any status string with no ownership check and no enum validation, writing directly into customer_orders.status — despite a code comment explicitly saying this route 'must not be open.'

Summary: [BE] Delivery/pickup OTP is brute-forceable
Description: verifyDeliveryOTP (537-567), verifyPickupCode (862-934) — 4-digit codes, no rate limiting, no lockout, directly gate 'proof of delivery.'

Summary: [BE] Driver broadcast searches from delivery address, not store
Description: shopkeeper.controller.ts's broadcastToNearbyDrivers() (~638-646) and delivery.controller.ts:187-212 both select customer_orders.delivery_latitude/delivery_longitude as the search center. A driver 'nearby' the delivery address may be far from the store the order needs picking up from first, and vice versa — drivers close to the store but far from the eventual drop-off never get offered the order.

Summary: [BE] delivery_fee/tip not verified server-side in checkout
Description: database.service.ts:722-1034. Client-sent delivery_fee is stored as-is; the frontend's real total also adds an optional customer tip not sent as its own field (pre-folded into order_total by CheckoutPage.tsx). Worst case bounded (skip the delivery charge, or under-total by a manipulated tip). Proper fix needs a frontend contract change (send tip_amount separately, backend independently computes distance-tiered delivery fee).

Summary: [BE] No admin UI to view/set master_products.gst_rate
Description: Confirmed via full grep of admin/src — zero references outside a read-only price-display calculation. The column is real, per-product, admin-controlled in the data model, but can only be set by hand in the Supabase table editor. Likely why most products currently have it unset/0.

Summary: [BE] Coupon usage_count increment is non-atomic
Description: database.service.ts:1199-1215 — concurrent checkouts can both pass the usage-limit check before either write lands, letting a usage_limit: 1 coupon be redeemed multiple times under burst traffic.

Summary: [BE] Google Places proxy is public with no rate limiting
Description: places.routes.ts (all 6 routes) — unlike the OTP endpoints, nothing throttles this; scriptable to run up Google Maps billing (cost-DoS).

Summary: [BE] getOrderById returns 500 instead of 404
Description: .single() throws before the !order check can run — database.service.ts:402-417 / orders.controller.ts:188-203. Worth a broader audit of .single() vs .maybeSingle() usage.

Summary: [BE] Admin coupon/delivery endpoints have no schema validation
Description: Unlike orders.routes.ts (uses validate(schema)) — coupons.controller.ts:29-49, delivery.controller.ts:50-75 pass req.body straight to the DB layer.

Summary: [BE] notifications.controller.ts has an IDOR pattern
Description: No req.customerId cross-check on userId/notificationId — currently harmless because the backing service (database.service.ts:1668-1687) is a complete stub. Will become exploitable the moment someone implements it without also adding the ownership check the route comment incorrectly claims already exists.

Summary: [BE] No notification when admin approves store/rider
Description: notification.service.ts only covers new-order events — approving a store or rider triggers no push/SMS/email; both mobile apps only find out via polling.

Summary: [BE] Legacy createOrder hardcodes Rs 20 delivery fee
Description: orders.controller.ts:89 — const PER_STORE_DELIVERY_FEE = 20, used regardless of actual distance. Distinct, older path from placeCheckoutOrder — customers on this path are charged a fee unrelated to real distance, diverging from the site's own distance-tier pricing.

Summary: [BE] GET /api/coupons has no active/expiry filter
Description: coupons.controller.ts:6-14 -> getCoupons() does a bare select('*'), no is_active/expiry predicate; filtered version only exists at /active. Now admin-only, which lowers severity, but admins still see expired/inactive coupons mixed into the main list.

Summary: [BE] acceptOffer's post-RPC follow-up writes are non-atomic
Description: deliveryPartner.controller.ts:836/844 — customer_orders and store_orders updates run after the atomic accept_driver_offer RPC, but aren't transactional with it or each other; a crash between them can leave the two tables disagreeing about who's assigned.

Summary: [BE] 3 duplicate haversine/distance implementations
Description: database.service.ts:1307 (calculateDistance), delivery.controller.ts:203 (inline haversine), shopkeeper.controller.ts:19 (haversineKm). No shared utility — any future fix has to be applied in three places independently.

Summary: [BE] No global Express error-handling middleware
Description: server.ts registers no 4-argument (err, req, res, next) handler anywhere; every route relies on its own try/catch, so an uncaught throw outside those crashes the request with Express's default error page instead of a controlled JSON response.

Summary: [BE] express.json() has no explicit body size limit
Description: server.ts:78 — app.use(express.json()) with no limit option. Relying on Express's implicit default rather than an intentional limit — plausible DoS vector via oversized payloads on public routes.

Summary: [BE] tracking_events table is fully dead, no inserts
Description: Confirmed via full grep of backend/src — the table exists in migrations and a route theoretically supports it, but only order_status_history is actually written.

Summary: [BE] Notification-preference endpoints are no-op stubs
Description: getNotificationPreferences/updateNotificationPreferences return fixed values / echo input without touching the DB. Narrow, low-traffic, but a real stub alongside the otherwise-real notification system.

Summary: [BE] getAgentSchedule ignores params, always returns []
Description: A stub, not wired to any real schedule data.

Summary: [BE] crypto.randomUUID() used without importing crypto
Description: auth.controller.ts:195,337 — works only because Node's global crypto happens to be available unprefixed; fragile across environments/bundlers.

Summary: [BE] sendSMS is a dead stub
Description: notification.service.ts:113-117 — always returns success without sending anything (// TODO: Twilio / AWS SNS).

Summary: [BE] requireShopkeeper blocks own profile for unapproved
Description: shopkeeper.controller.ts:30-71 — a newly-signed-up shopkeeper can't check their own approval status.

Summary: [BE] Expo push sends never inspect per-message errors
Description: delivery.controller.ts:232-241, shopkeeper.controller.ts:499-511,550-560 — stale/invalid tokens never get cleaned up, failures are invisible.

Summary: [BE] Duplicate Express Request augmentation in invoice.controller
Description: invoice.controller.ts:9-15, already redundant with 3 other files — the file's own comments document this exact duplication caused a real bug once before.

Summary: [BE] getProductById fetches whole catalog then .find()s
Description: products.controller.ts:51-66 — databaseService.getProductsWithDetails() (no filter) then linear-scans for the one product. Latency/cost concern as the catalog grows.

Summary: [BE] getNearbyStores does a full scan + JS-side filter
Description: database.service.ts:283-298 — select('*').eq('is_active', true) over all stores, then filtered by distance in JS. No PostGIS/SQL-side geo filter, on a hot customer-facing path.

Summary: [BE] getProductsWithDetails does full-scan + JS filter
Description: database.service.ts:245-281 — queries unfiltered by location, then filters by calculateDistance() in JS when a radius is requested.

Summary: [Web] Razorpay amount fully client-computed, no recheck
Description: pages/CheckoutPage.tsx:427 -> services/paymentGateway.ts:76-89 posts a client-computed amount to /api/payment/create. Combined with backend order-total trust issues, there's no verified guardrail preventing a manipulated total from reaching payment creation.

Summary: [Web] Direct-from-browser order-write fallback path
Description: utils/apiBase.ts:18-21 (shouldUseBackendApi) + services/supabase.ts:483-750 (createOrder) — if VITE_API_URL is ever unset in production, the app falls back to writing customer_orders/store_orders/order_items directly from the browser via an anon-key client, letting the client set order_total/discount_amount/payment_status directly, relying entirely on unverified RLS.

Summary: [Web] Logout doesn't clear the cart
Description: context/AuthContext.tsx:166-186 (logoutUser) never calls clearCart(). Shared/kiosk device: User B logs in after User A logs out and sees/can check out User A's cart. LocationContext's saved location is likewise never cleared.

Summary: [Web] No global 401 handling on the customer site
Description: Only ShopkeeperApp.tsx/DriverApp.tsx check status === 401; the actual customer flow never detects an expired/revoked session, clears it, and redirects to login — user gets stuck with isAuthenticated: true but every API call silently failing.

Summary: [Web] useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts stale closure bug
Description: Lines 64-96,99-147 — the realtime-subscription effect's dependency array is [orderId, !!order] (a boolean), not [orderId, order], so the closure keeps referencing the first render's order object. Any order field not explicitly re-assigned in each update permanently reverts to its first-render value.

Summary: [Web] GSTIN/business name discarded from order payload
Description: pages/CheckoutPage.tsx:80-82 (state) / :451-474 (orderData — no gstin/businessName field). Business customers filling this in get no GST record on their invoice. Distinct from the mobile app's version, already fixed.

Summary: [Web] Invoice download sends raw UUID as Bearer token
Description: pages/OrdersPage.tsx:251-254 — Authorization: Bearer ${user.id}. Fragile/misleading auth pattern; would be directly exploitable if requireCustomer ever regressed to accepting bare UUIDs.

Summary: [Web] Realtime + unconditional 3s poll run simultaneously
Description: hooks/useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts:141 — setInterval(refreshOrderAndHistory, 3000) runs alongside the realtime channel with no health-gating, doubling backend load for no benefit when realtime is healthy.

Summary: [Web] No AbortController on in-flight tracking fetches
Description: useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts:64-96,154-165 — stale responses can land and overwrite state after unmount/navigation or after a newer request already resolved.

Summary: [Web] Driver location polled every 2s unconditionally
Description: useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts:151-165, no gating on realtime connection state — the website's own version of an already-documented customer-mobile-app bug.

Summary: [Web] Toast auto-dismiss timers reset on any list change
Description: NotificationContext.tsx:34-48 — bursts of notifications within the dismiss window can pile up or overstay far longer than configured.

Summary: [Web] HomePage doesn't re-fetch on delivery location change
Description: Unlike ShopPage.tsx, which correctly does — stale 'nearby store' listing until a full reload.

Summary: [Web] SearchPage has no request cancellation guard
Description: SearchPage.tsx:15-39 — rapid searches can have an older, slower response overwrite a newer search's results.

Summary: [Web] ThankYouPage has no fallback fetch by order ID
Description: ThankYouPage.tsx:10-17,131-158 — order details come only from React Router location.state; refreshing the post-payment page (very common) loses all context and shows 'Order ID: N/A'.

Summary: [Web] Two parallel admin-auth systems referenced from frontend
Description: secureAdminAuth.ts's Edge-Function JWT login is referenced by several components but the real login flow (AdminLoginPage.tsx) uses a completely different mechanism (adminAuthService.ts) — the 'secure' path is dead code that's easy to mistake for what's actually running.

Summary: [Web] API-base-URL resolution duplicated in 3 places
Description: utils/apiBase.ts, services/authService.ts:44-52, services/orderService.ts:4-12 — instead of sharing one implementation, drift risk.

Summary: [Web] utils/encryption.ts dead code, weak fallback key
Description: Unused anywhere; client-side 'encryption' with a bundle-embedded key (defaulting to a hardcoded weak string if unset) provides no real confidentiality even if wired up.

Summary: [Web] Checkout email field is required
Description: pages/CheckoutPage.tsx:813 — blocks OTP customers with no real email from completing checkout, even though customer_email is optional server-side.

Summary: [Web] No empty-cart redirect at checkout
Description: CheckoutPage.tsx has no mount-time guard, only a submit-time check — landing directly on /checkout with an empty cart shows a fully rendered, unusable form instead of redirecting.

Summary: [Web] getCurrentUserFromSession uses anon client, blocked by RLS
Description: services/authService.ts:183-215. Masked for the common case by AuthContext.tsx restoring from localStorage on mount, but any path needing a genuine fresh server read silently falls back to stale local state.

Summary: [Web] CartContext.addToCart mutates cart item in place
Description: context/CartContext.tsx:121-125 — updatedItems[existingItemIndex].quantity += quantity mutates the same object referenced in prevItems. Latent bug for any React.memo-wrapped cart row.

Summary: [Web] OrderTrackingPage invoice link 404s
Description: pages/OrderTrackingPage.tsx:738-746 links to /api/invoices/${orderId} (doesn't exist); real routes are /api/invoices/order/:orderId/{customer,store,delivery}. Always 404s.

Summary: [Web] ProtectedRoute.tsx is dead code
Description: No importer anywhere; CheckoutPage, OrdersPage, and others each hand-roll a slightly different inline auth-redirect guard instead, producing inconsistent UX.

Summary: [Web] Stale/undocumented Vite env vars
Description: Root .env.example:7 still documents VITE_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (nothing reads it anymore, misleading toward re-introducing the already-fixed key-leak risk); VITE_ENCRYPTION_KEY is undocumented.

Summary: [Web] GSTIN 'claim GST credit' copy is misleading
Description: CheckoutPage.tsx:1100 — promises a benefit that can't happen since GSTIN is dropped from the payload. Real tax/accounting consequence for business customers.

Summary: [Web] O(n^3) store-assignment algorithm in createOrder
Description: Uses items.indexOf() inside nested loops — harmless at typical cart sizes, but wasteful.

Summary: [Web] 60+ files have unstripped console.log in production
Description: Including full order payloads and user IDs/phones.

Summary: [Web] utils/dataMasking.ts is confirmed dead code
Description: Zero importers.

Summary: [Web] Security utilities applied only to admin surface
Description: sanitize.ts, csrf.ts, rateLimit.ts — never to customer-facing forms — inconsistent application, though no direct injection sink found on the customer side.

Summary: [Admin] Admin login reads password_hash via anon-readable RLS
Description: services/adminAuthService.ts:85-104 (authenticateAdmin) calls getAdminClient() before any session exists, falling back to the anon-key client — then does bcrypt.compare() in the browser against a hash fetched over the public anon key. Anyone can dump every admin's password hash for offline cracking if the admins RLS policy still permits anon SELECT.

Summary: [Admin] getAdminClient() silently downgrades to anon client
Description: services/supabase.ts:46-55 — every function in adminAuthService.ts, adminService.ts, auditLog.ts, and every page calling getAdminClient() directly runs as fully anonymous the moment the session token is gone, with no error thrown.

Summary: [Admin] Admin creation has no server-side role check or RLS
Description: Confirmed via full grep of supabase/migrations/*.sql — zero RLS hits on admins. createAdmin() (adminAuthService.ts:190-227) does a plain insert with nothing at the DB layer stopping arbitrary role/permission assignment.

Summary: [Admin] Unchecked-mutation bug class in Notifications/AdminHeader
Description: NotificationsPage.tsx:221-239 (markAllRead/markOneRead/deleteNotification) and :76-82 (PushNotificationPanel.handleSend), plus AdminHeader.tsx:118-120 (markAllRead) — all call .update()/.delete()/.insert() against admin_notifications with no error or row-count check, then optimistically update local state regardless of success.

Summary: [Admin] Store approval is 'blind' — no verification docs shown
Description: StoresPage.tsx never selects/renders verification_document, verification_number, or owner_image_url — columns that exist and are already shown for delivery partners. Admin can click Approve without ever seeing the shopkeeper's submitted ID.

Summary: [Admin] PushNotificationPanel never actually sends a push
Description: NotificationsPage.tsx handleSend (~57-100) fetches Expo push tokens, then only inserts an admin_notifications log row and reports success — there is no actual Expo push API call anywhere in the function. Admins believe a broadcast went out when none did.

Summary: [Admin] No per-endpoint permission enforcement for admin roles
Description: hasPermission() (adminAuthService.ts:293-309) is only checked from React components; nothing at the RLS or backend-middleware layer enforces role. A viewer-role admin can bypass the UI to perform writes their role shouldn't allow.

Summary: [Admin] Password-strength schemas defined but never used
Description: admin/src/schemas/admin.schema.ts:11-39,41-68 enforce upper/lower/digit/special-char, but CreateAdminPage.tsx:65 / EditAdminPage.tsx:110 only check length < 8 — weak 8-character passwords like 'aaaaaaaa' are accepted for admin accounts.

Summary: [Admin] Session-creation writes in authenticateAdmin unchecked
Description: adminAuthService.ts:112-127 — the admin_sessions insert and last_login_at update results are discarded entirely. If the session insert silently fails, the UI hands back a token that looks valid but has no corresponding DB row.

Summary: [Admin] updateCustomerStatus is a dead no-op
Description: adminService.ts:802-811 — takes unused params, logs a warning, returns null without touching the DB. Currently unused by any button, but a trap if resurrected.

Summary: [Admin] AdminSidebar isActive('/') references dead path
Description: References /admin, but the app is mounted at / — the Dashboard nav item can never show as active. Cosmetic.

Summary: [Admin] navigator.userAgent called unguarded in auditLog.ts
Description: services/auditLog.ts:37,62,78 — no environment guard (unlike adminAuthService.ts:119, which has one). If run outside a browser context, throws and silently drops the audit-log write.

Summary: [Admin] getCustomers returns all app_users with no role filter
Description: adminService.ts:703-706 — no .eq('role', 'customer') — non-customer roles would leak into the Customers list/count.

Summary: [Admin] Order-status mapping collapses statuses into 5 states
Description: mapDbStatusToFrontend, adminService.ts:447-454 — e.g. preparing_order and ready_for_pickup both collapse to 'confirmed', losing operationally-relevant granularity.

Summary: [Admin] Admin updateOrderStatus can never set preparing_order
Description: adminService.ts:662-668 maps 'confirmed' -> 'store_accepted', skipping over preparing_order entirely. Distinct from the missing state-machine-validation bug in the same function.

Summary: [Admin] Admin session stored in sessionStorage only
Description: AdminLoginPage.tsx:46-48 — closing the tab silently logs the admin out, no persistent option.

Summary: [Admin] Settings prefs are localStorage-only, don't sync
Description: SettingsPage.tsx (NOTIF_PREFS_KEY ~215-268, DISPLAY_PREFS_KEY ~272-339) — don't follow the admin across devices; only Account/password does a real DB write.

Summary: [Admin] Client-side-only rate limiting on admin login
Description: utils/rateLimit.ts — an in-memory Map reset by any page refresh, no real protection for the vulnerable login flow.

Summary: [Admin] Admin email/role/error-stack logged to console
Description: On every login attempt, uncompressed for production.

Summary: [Admin] services/supabase.ts has dead-weight customer logic
Description: createOrder, getUserOrders, newsletter, address CRUD — dead weight in the admin bundle, looks like a stale copy-paste from the customer-facing frontend.

Summary: [Admin] sessionStorage['adminData'] parsed with raw JSON.parse
Description: CreateAdminPage.tsx:33-34, EditAdminPage.tsx:38-39, AdminManagementPage.tsx:125-126 — instead of the shared getCurrentAdmin() helper — any corruption crashes these pages with an unhandled exception.

Summary: [DB] Migrations can't bootstrap schema from an empty DB
Description: Grepping every CREATE TABLE for coupons, app_users, stores, admins, categories, master_products, order_items, store_orders, and 12 other tables returns zero matches for all of them — only delivery_partners has a real tracked CREATE TABLE. supabase db reset or a fresh project from these migrations alone would fail immediately.

Summary: [DB] customer_orders.payment_method type never created
Description: 20260404120000_customer_orders_razorpay_payment_id.sql:109 declares type public.payment_method_type, but the enum actually created later is named public.payment_method (no _type suffix). Replayed purely from tracked migrations, this fails.

Summary: [DB] public.order_status enum has no CREATE TYPE
Description: The single most important enum in the app (drives all order lifecycle transitions) — used from 20260404120000 onward and later ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE'd, but never created.

Summary: [DB] SECURITY DEFINER functions missing SET search_path
Description: accept_driver_offer(), auto_offline_stale_drivers(), is_account_locked(), is_admin_authenticated(), notify_admin_new_order(), notify_admin_new_user(), debug_admin_headers(). is_admin_authenticated() is the most sensitive — sole gate for every admin_full_access RLS policy across 9 tables. Standard Postgres privilege-escalation hardening gap.

Summary: [DB] debug_admin_headers() diagnostic function never dropped
Description: Despite its own comment saying 'safe to drop once resolved' (it has been). SECURITY DEFINER, no search_path pin, granted to anon/authenticated, returns the full matching admin_sessions row (including session_token) to any caller with a valid x-admin-token.

Summary: [DB] No trigger forcing stores.is_active false on revoke
Description: The rider side has an equivalent trigger; the store side doesn't, so a store stays online until the shopkeeper (or a future toggle) happens to touch the row.

Summary: [DB] Public-read grants trap on coupons/product tables
Description: SELECT granted to anon/authenticated while RLS stays disabled on those tables — appears deliberate and carefully scoped, but worth a second confirmation pass and updating SCHEMA_NOTES.md.

Summary: [DB] 3 RLS policies are permanently dead code
Description: customer_own_orders, customer_own_record, partner_own_record — gate on auth.uid(), but this app doesn't use Supabase Auth, so auth.uid() is always NULL. Not exploitable (fails closed), but misleading.

Summary: [Store] Push notifications never initialized at app startup
Description: lib/notifications.ts's notificationService.initialize() has no caller anywhere in the app — the only way a token gets registered is manually opening Settings -> Notifications -> Enable, despite copy implying it's on by default.

Summary: [Store] No global 401 handling
Description: lib/api-client.ts's request() never inspects 401s to trigger logout; error-handler.ts has a handleAuthError() that's dead code. Every screen shows empty data indefinitely on an expired session.

Summary: [Store] clearSession() doesn't clear the store-list cache
Description: session.ts:56-63 never calls clearStoreCache() — real cross-account data leak window on shared devices; shopkeeper B briefly sees A's store name/address/approval status.

Summary: [Store] Push token never cleared on logout
Description: Same root cause as the initialize() gap — a shared device could keep delivering one shopkeeper's order notifications to whoever's currently logged in.

Summary: [Store] useRequireStoreApproval() is reactive, not preventive
Description: The redirect only fires after an async round-trip resolves; Orders/Payouts/Inventory data-fetching effects run in parallel unconditioned on the approval check. If the request errors, the guard fails open silently forever.

Summary: [Store] Orphaned inventory.tsx route has no approval guard
Description: Still deep-link-reachable via Expo Router's file-based routing even though no in-app navigation targets it; writes directly to the DB.

Summary: [Store] Realtime channel names include Date.now()
Description: home.tsx:216,223 — causes churn on every store-selection/re-render instead of reusing/deduping channels.

Summary: [Store] Auto-complete-order-after-2-min only while app open
Description: previous-orders.tsx:285-305 — if the shopkeeper backgrounds/closes the app, orders can be stuck un-completed indefinitely; should be a server-side scheduled job.

Summary: [Store] 2 dead-code order-management implementations
Description: hooks/useOrders.ts + components/IncomingOrderPopup.tsx (never wired in) and lib/order-service.ts + components/OrderFilters.tsx (targets a nonexistent API contract).

Summary: [Store] AppContext.tsx and testSupabase.ts are dead code
Description: testSupabase.ts includes a function that performs live inserts/deletes against the real products table.

Summary: [Store] CachedStore type doesn't declare is_approved
Description: All the approval-gate logic throughout the app relies on untyped 'as any' casts — a backend field rename would fail silently with no compiler error.

Summary: [Store] Home screen activeOrderCount hardcoded to 0
Description: app/(tabs)/home.tsx:109 — const activeOrderCount = 0, passed straight into StoreStatusCard. Shopkeepers see a permanently-wrong indicator.

Summary: [Store] Store images support only a single image
Description: lib/storage.ts:71-77 uses a fixed path and returns a single result; real product-completeness gap for store listings.

Summary: [Store] No admin notification on profile-edit (approval flow)
Description: Zero matches for a profile_change_request concept anywhere in the repo — profile edits take effect with no admin review trail.

Summary: [Store] toggleStoreStatus implemented 3x, only 1 wired up
Description: lib/store-service.ts:146, hooks/useStore.ts:75, and an inline copy in home.tsx:252-275 (the only one wired). A fix applied to either unused implementation would silently never take effect.

Summary: [Store] setAllProductsOffline/restoreActiveProductsOnline no-op
Description: lib/storeProducts.ts:436-438,444-446 both return true unconditionally, yet are awaited unconditionally on every toggle in home.tsx. Gives shopkeepers a false impression that product visibility changes.

Summary: [Store] orders-db.ts has a silent 4-strategy fallback chain
Description: getOrdersFromDb() cascades RPC -> direct-table -> customer_orders fallback, all failures swallowed to console.warn. An RLS misconfiguration degrades silently into an indistinguishable 'no orders' state.

Summary: [Store] Own supabase/*.sql scripts unverifiable on live DB
Description: Tracked in git but aren't real Supabase-CLI migrations (no migrations/ dir or config.toml) — several live code paths depend on these having been run against production with no static way to confirm.

Summary: [Store] Silent catch {} blocks in approval-check/session flows
Description: Already partly known/intentional, re-confirmed present.

Summary: [Store] Heavy unconditional console.log in lib/notifications.ts
Description: Not gated behind __DEV__, including full notification payload contents.

Summary: [Store] scheduleNotification's _triggerDate param ignored
Description: Always fires immediately regardless of what's passed — misleading if a 'daily summary' feature is ever built on top.

Summary: [Store] registration-success.tsx redirects after flat 2.5s
Description: Regardless of outcome — no immediate 'you're pending' moment right after signup.

Summary: [Store] isActivated defaults to true when missing from server
Description: Fail-open, though currently only affects a profile-page label, not access control.

Summary: [Rider] Service-role key plumbed into the client bundle
Description: constants/config.ts:33-37 + app.config.js:86-90 — if EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is ever set at build time, the full-privilege key ships inside every installed APK/IPA, extractable via decompilation.

Summary: [Rider] Unauthenticated fallback leaks every rider's PII
Description: app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:110-135 — if /delivery-partner/profile fails, falls back to fetching the entire delivery-partner roster with no token, exposing every other rider's verification documents, phone, and address.

Summary: [Rider] 'Always' location requested but never used
Description: app.config.js configures always-permission, but code only calls requestForegroundPermissionsAsync() and uses foreground-only watchPositionAsync + a JS setInterval heartbeat. A rider mid-delivery with a locked phone has stale location while still being dispatched new offers.

Summary: [Rider] Reinstatement invisible until app restart
Description: The status-approval poll only starts when driverStatus === 'pending_verification' — a rider reinstated while the app stays open has no way to discover it short of force-quitting.

Summary: [Rider] Approval detection is poll-only, no realtime
Description: Unlike order offers, which do use Realtime. A newly-signed-up rider waits up to 30s after admin approval to see it reflected.

Summary: [Rider] No ErrorBoundary anywhere in the app
Description: An unhandled render exception crashes the whole app with no fallback UI.

Summary: [Rider] Double-tap 'Accept offer' can fire two requests
Description: No useRef in-flight lock, only async state — server-side atomicity likely prevents double-award, but produces duplicate network calls/alerts/navigations.

Summary: [Rider] Realtime-channel subscribe/teardown race
Description: home.tsx:321-356 can orphan a Supabase channel subscription if isOnline toggles quickly — leads to duplicated haptic buzzes/fetches for new offers.

Summary: [Rider] Earnings screen hardcodes a 15% commission client-side
Description: profile.tsx:147, earnings.tsx:90-98,163,217 — no backend-provided payout field; will silently drift from real payouts if the commission structure ever varies.

Summary: [Rider] 'Ignore offer' is client-state-only, unpersisted
Description: Resets on any app restart, so a previously-ignored offer can reappear.

Summary: [Rider] Active-order poll runs 6s regardless of online status
Description: app/(tabs)/home.tsx:319-342 — gated only on !token, unconditionally polls. Only the offers poll is gated on isOnline — unnecessary battery/network drain while deliberately offline.

Summary: [Rider] No admin audit trail on rider profile-edit requests
Description: app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:212-244 does a direct PATCH with no request-tracking or admin-facing notification.

Summary: [Rider] Rider rating hardcoded at 4.8
Description: app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:354 — literal 4.8, no backend-sourced field. Displays a fabricated trust/quality signal.

Summary: [Rider] No vehicle type/image fields in rider profile UI
Description: Backend already returns vehicle_number but nothing displays it — incomplete KYC/profile data surface.

Summary: [Rider] No document image upload for rider verification
Description: app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:369-388 — Verification Document/Number render as plain read-only text, no image picker/upload UI. Riders can't submit/update KYC documents.

Summary: [Rider] Orders screen status badges mismatch backend
Description: app/(tabs)/orders.tsx:30-34 defines a badge for en_route_delivery (dead); picking_up/picked_up (which the backend does emit) have no badge config, falling through to unstyled generic rendering.

Summary: [Rider] lib/authService.ts is dead code with a PII-logs risk
Description: Never imported; its unused OTP functions log raw phone numbers and API URLs — latent risk if ever wired back in.

Summary: [Rider] MapViewWrapper.tsx is unused dead weight
Description: The app bundles react-native-maps and Google Maps keys for no actual in-app map usage.

Summary: [Rider] State-instead-of-ref double-submit race in otp.tsx
Description: Same pattern as the accept-offer bug above — low-likelihood double-submit race.

Summary: [Rider] isActivated session field threaded but dead/unused
Description: Easy to mistake for the real approval gate.

Summary: [Rider] Hardcoded admin support phone number in home.tsx
Description: Rather than sourced from config.

Summary: [Cust] Logout doesn't clear cart/push token
Description: One of 6 previously-known issues, still open.

Summary: [Cust] trackingService treats any failure as 'Order not found'
Description: One of 6 previously-known issues, still open.

Summary: [Cust] Cold-start push deep links can be dropped
Description: One of 6 previously-known issues, still open.

Summary: [Cust] No push-token unregister on logout
Description: One of 6 previously-known issues, still open.

Summary: [Cust] Dead 'Order Placed!' overlay in checkout.tsx
Description: One of 6 previously-known issues, still open.

Summary: [Cust] Service-role key still required for 4 files
Description: lib/supabase.ts:31-54 builds an admin client from a service-role key; addressService.ts, authService.ts, product/coupons.tsx, distanceUtils.ts all still depend on it. Either the key ships inside every installed app (full DB compromise) or these features are broken in production. Needs an architecture fix routing through the authenticated backend. (Order placement's piece of this is already fixed.)

Summary: [Cust] IDOR on authService.ts/addressService.ts
Description: Both take a bare userId string with zero check it matches the authenticated session. Anyone can query any other customer's full profile and saved addresses by ID alone. (Order placement's piece of this is already fixed; read-only order-history/status lookups also still open.)

Summary: [Cust] Cart is never cleared after a successful order
Description: COD or paid — the only clearCart() call site is in the failed/cancelled payment branch. A customer can accidentally re-order the same items.

Summary: [Cust] No cart quantity ceiling or stock check anywhere
Description: CartContext.tsx places no upper bound on quantity, and createOrder never validates against any inventory field before inserting.

Summary: [Cust] discount_amount hardcoded to 0 on every order
Description: orderService.ts:375 — regardless of an applied coupon; the discount is folded into total_amount, but the dedicated finance/reporting column is always wrong.

Summary: [Cust] Coupon expiry is fetched but never enforced
Description: Client or server side — an expired-but-still-active coupon remains applicable with zero re-validation at order-creation time.

Summary: [Cust] Google Maps key called directly, bypassing backend proxy
Description: Confirmed direct calls to maps.googleapis.com at multiple screens; backend/src/routes/places.routes.ts already provides an equivalent proxy and is never called. Ships a billable, extractable key inside every installed app.

Summary: [Cust] Invoice screen is fabricated client-side; PDF is a stub
Description: app/order/invoice/[id].tsx:38-54 builds a fake invoice locally, never calling the real backend invoice endpoint. Download shows a 'coming in next update' alert. Customers cannot get a real tax invoice from the app.

Summary: [Cust] Widespread fire-and-forget .catch(() => {}) patterns
Description: Across cache writes and order-placed flags — no logging/telemetry means cache desyncs are invisible in production.

Summary: [Cust] Order-tracking runs 5s+2s polls alongside realtime
Description: Doubling backend load for no benefit when realtime is healthy.

Summary: [Cust] Order-confirmation scans full order list, not by ID
Description: Rather than fetching it directly — degrades to a silent empty state if the new order hasn't propagated into the cached list.

Summary: [Cust] GSTIN input in checkout has no format validation
Description: Before being persisted and later printed on the customer's own tax invoice.

Summary: [Cust] Wallet screen is a fully non-functional shell
Description: app/wallet.tsx:37-45 — 'Add Money' just shows a coming-soon alert; balance is hardcoded to Rs 0.00.

Summary: [Cust] Saved-payment-methods feature is unimplemented
Description: app/support/payment-options.tsx:112-137 — nothing to display until the backend wires up Razorpay Customer tokens.

Summary: [Cust] Payment reconcile loop has no circuit breaker
Description: hooks/usePaymentFlow.ts:59-73 loops the full 10s window and only short-circuits on 'paid', never on cancelled/failed.

Summary: [Cust] Hardcoded Rs 25 delivery fee, distance data unused
Description: constants/fees.ts:3,7-9 — real distance computation only used for a UI warning, never fed into the fee calculation. Customer-app twin of the already-fixed website bug.

Summary: [Cust] 29+ more console.log/warn/error call sites
Description: Beyond what's already known, no structured error tracking.

Summary: [Cust] lib/supabase.ts logs CRITICAL warning in production
Description: If credentials are missing, rather than failing fast/reporting to a tracker.

Summary: [Cust] invoiceService.ts hardcodes placeholder GSTIN/phone
Description: As a silent fallback if business-info fetch fails — could ship on a real customer invoice.

Summary: [Cust] Biased shuffle used for 'suggested products'
Description: Array.sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5) — harmless here, but a correctness smell if copied elsewhere.

Summary: [Infra] createPaymentOrder tests fail (fetch mock leaks)
Description: res.text is not a function — vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...) replaces fetch process-wide, so an earlier real Supabase call inside createPaymentOrder also gets intercepted by a mock shaped only for the Razorpay response. Needs the test to mock the Supabase call separately.

Summary: [Infra] invoice.test.ts fails on a setTimeout timing bug
Description: downloadHtml() wraps URL.revokeObjectURL(url) in setTimeout(..., 100); the test asserts immediately with no fake-timer advance or await. Not a product bug — the app's actual download behavior is unaffected.

Summary: [Infra] Admin panel has no test infrastructure at all
Description: No test script, no test files — zero automated coverage on the app that gates every store/rider approval and holds the client-side admin login.

Summary: [Infra] Rider app: 2 ESLint errors, missing displayName
Description: components/MapViewWrapper.tsx:14,20 — both component definitions missing displayName (react/display-name). Low impact since the file is already flagged as unused dead weight.

Summary: [Infra] Rider app: 18 exhaustive-deps lint warnings
Description: Missing animation-ref dependencies across otp.tsx, signup.tsx, home.tsx, orders.tsx, profile.tsx, _layout.tsx, index.tsx, phone.tsx, earnings.tsx, delivery/[orderId].tsx, OfflineBanner.tsx. Cosmetic/intentional in most cases, not investigated file-by-file.

Summary: [Infra] Rider app has no test infrastructure
Description: No test script/files.

Summary: [Infra] Customer app: expo lint fails outright
Description: 'No files matching the pattern .../src' — 0 files ever scanned. Traced to a stray, empty, git-untracked src/screens/ directory on the affected machine; expo lint defaults to scanning src/ when present. Fix: delete the empty local src/ directory.

Summary: [Infra] Customer app has no test infrastructure
Description: No test script/files — same gap as admin and rider app.
